OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL DARYL WASHINGTON TO ANSWER QUESTIONS AT DEPOSITION AND TO APPEAR FOR A FURTHER DEPOSITION FILED BY PLAINTIFF WASHINGTON, DARYL

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO FILM SOCIETY AND GRAHAM LEGGAT'S' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO ANSWER QUESTIONS AT DEPOSITION AND TO APPEAR FOR FURTHER DEPOSITION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO GIVE A WRITTEN RESPONSE SETTING FORTH HIS RESIDENTIAL ADDRESS. WHILE ARGUABLY PLAINTIFF HAS UNDERTAKEN A COURSE OF CONDUCT DESIGNED TO MAINTAIN THE CONFIDENTIALITY OF HIS ADDRESS, THERE IS NO RIGHT TO PRIVACY IN THIS LITIGATION TO THAT INFORMATION. THERE IS NO NEED FOR ADDITIONAL ORAL TESTIMONY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Daryl Washington To Answer Questions At Deposition And To Appear Questions At Deposition And To Appear For A Further Depositons; Statement Of Disputed Questions And Answers At Deposition
Off Calendar Aug-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED.

DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO FILM SOCIETY and GRAHAM LEGGAT'S' Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Answer Questions At Deposition And To Appear For Further Deposition is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is ordered to give a written response setting forth his residential address. While arguably Plaintiff has undertaken a course of conduct designed to maintain the confidentiality of his address, there is no right to privacy in this litigation to that information. There is no need for additional oral testimony. Prevailing party to submit a form of order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
